strengths: Clear identification of three specific objects with precise real-world contexts (Stonehenge; self-produced painting; photographed quotation).
Consistent, coherent links drawn between each object and Prompt 18 on unknowability, demonstrating understanding of TOK concepts.
Justifications for each objectâs inclusion are generally well supported by relevant examples and brief scholarly references.
Structure and signposting (e.g. clear headings for each object) enhance the exhibitionâs clarity and logical flow.
weaknesses: Analysis occasionally drifts into descriptive narrative with unsubstantiated assertions (e.g. on âpropositional knowledge not yet justifiedâ).
Evidence is uneven: later objects rely heavily on anecdotal reflection rather than tighter conceptual framing.
Some TOK terminology and counter-arguments are underdefined or missing, reducing analytical precision.
Presentation details (citations, formatting, captions, concluding synthesis) require refinement to meet academic rigour and IB standards.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission meets the descriptors for the 7â8 band: it clearly identifies three well-contextualised objects and makes strong, coherent connections to the IA prompt, supported by relevant examples. There is a strong justification for each objectâs contribution to the theme of unknowability, and the student demonstrates good use of TOK vocabulary and concepts. However, occasional lapses into narrative description, uneven evidential support for later objects, and some formatting and citation inconsistencies prevent it from reaching the top 9â10 band. An 8 accurately reflects the overall quality against the strand assessments while respecting the examinerâs final mark.

annotations.comment: The prompt is clearly restated at the start, ensuring alignment with the IA focus on unknowability.
Consider explicitly referencing âPrompt 18â or its full wording in your introduction to reinforce the link to the IA. This clarifies your focus from the outset.
The heading âObject 1: Stone circle of Stonehengeâ succinctly identifies the first object and sets up its discussion effectively.
Good contextualisation of Stonehenge on Salisbury Plain situates the object in a precise realâworld setting.
The parenthetical citation â(Pearson)â lacks a date or page number. Use a consistent inâtext citation format (e.g., APA or MLA) to strengthen academic rigour.
The claim that âknowledge does not have to be fully justifiedâ oversimplifies TOKâs nuanced view of justification. Consider acknowledging counterarguments or definitions from TOK literature.
Strengthen the link to the IA prompt by explicitly using the term âunknowableâ when discussing theorizing about the pastâthis will reinforce your central TOK theme.
The final sentence in this paragraph is incomplete, suggesting missing text. Ensure each thought is fully articulated to maintain coherence.
The transition to Object 2 on this page is wellâsignposted, maintaining a clear structure.
When introducing the painting, briefly restate its specific realâworld context (e.g., school presentation) here to keep each objectâs context immediately visible.
Combining propositional, procedural, and personal knowledge in the paintingâs creation provides a strong TOK insight into multiple ways of knowing.
The argument on personal thoughts being unknowable is repetitive. Consolidate this point and tie it more directly back to Prompt 18 to avoid redundancy.
Excellent use of a peer interpretation anecdote to illustrate how artwork meaning varies across knowersâthis underscores unknowability well.
The anecdote about the tear symbol could be linked to broader TOK theory (e.g., shared interpretive frameworks) to deepen the analysis.
Object 3 is clearly identified along with its realâworld source, maintaining the required structure for each object.
Add a brief descriptive caption below the quotation image to explain its relevance and guide the readerâs interpretation.
Contextualising the novel quote within its fictional setting effectively shows how imagined certainty contrasts with realâworld unknowability.
The reference to âcertainty these teenagers count onâ is vague. Specify how this fictional certainty relates to TOK concepts like propositional knowledge.
Identifying the limitations of predicting future events demonstrates awareness of the nature of knowledge across communities of knowers.
The extensive plot paraphrase reduces analytical focus. Prioritise concise TOK discussion over narrative summary to maintain depth.
Your claim about âpropositional knowledge not yet justifiedâ needs clear definitions and references to TOK terminology. Define justification within your analysis.
The works cited entry for Britannica is clear, but ensure book and website titles are consistently formatted (e.g., italics for titles).
The citation for âI fell in love with hopeâ omits publisher location and standard punctuation. Complete this entry for MLA consistency.
Include an episode or chapter reference for âOutlanderâ to clarify the exact source of your âmystical gatewaysâ example.
Add a concluding synthesis at the end of your exhibition to tie together how all three objects collectively address the unknowability prompt.
Label the Stonehenge image with its source and date directly beneath for clarity and to meet exhibition standards.
For the painting image, include the creation date and medium (e.g., acrylic on canvas, 2023) to situate it in its specific realâworld context.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
